title: zanthoxylum monogynum subsp. bolivianum
intro: Zanthoxylum monogynum subsp. bolivianum is a subspecies of plant in the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the Bolivian pepper tree or Bolivian prickly ash. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation due to its compound salicin, similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum monogynum subsp. bolivianum include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, which is used to relieve pain and reduce fever.
